summary: 2026-06-05 23:13:20 PDT — Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/harden-merge-and-guard.md`. The close recipe and docs mostly match the spec, but the guard still misses a standard `--force-with-lease=<ref>` form. The full guard test could not run in this read-only sandbox because `mktemp` could not create its temp repo, but targeted hook payloads confirmed this bypass.

findings.title: Guard misses --force-with-lease value form
findings.claim: AC6 requires the guard to block force pushes via `--force-with-lease`, but the new detector only matches exact tokens in `FORCE_FLAGS`. Git also accepts `--force-with-lease=<ref>` / `--force-with-lease=<ref>:<expect>`; a payload like `git push --force-with-lease=main origin main` exits 0 on this branch, so a normal force-with-lease push can bypass the guard from a feature branch.
findings.suggestion: Treat long force options as blocked when they are exact matches or start with `--force-with-lease=` (and any other supported `--force...=` forms you intend to cover), then add a guard fixture for `git push --force-with-lease=main origin main`.
